title: elymus elongatus subsp. haifensis
intro: Elymus elongatus subsp. haifensis is a sub-species of the Elymus elongatus plant, commonly known as the "woody blue grama" or "woody grama," a type of perennial grass native to the Middle East and North Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Elymus elongatus subsp. haifensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
